behaviors.description: The primary translator produces a grammatically coherent translation that preserves the meaning of the source text, not just a word-for-word substitution.
behaviors.justification: Top choice: Agentic. Runner-up: Integration. Rejected Integration because the behavior explicitly tests output quality: "grammatically coherent" and "preserves the meaning". A stubbed translator would hide real model-quality failures; correctness depends on real NMT judgments. Rejected Workflow because this is a single model decision, not multiple agentic steps.
behaviors.test_description: Call the real translation model on a representative multilingual evaluation set and assert adequacy/fluency thresholds (semantic preservation + grammar quality) against reference expectations.

behaviors.description: When the translation API returns a 503 error, the service transparently falls back to the dictionary-based translator without returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Top choice: Integration. Runner-up: System. Rejected System because the failure can be reproduced by module handoff with an in-process API client stub returning 503; behavior text is about fallback orchestration: "falls back ... without returning an error". No real OS/network semantics are required to trigger this branch. Rejected Agentic because correctness does not depend on model output content.
behaviors.test_description: Wire service + fallback translator + SQLite-backed dictionary with an API client stub that returns 503; assert caller gets successful fallback translation and no propagated error.

behaviors.description: The dictionary-based fallback returns the correct pre-stored translation for phrases that exist in the SQLite database.
behaviors.justification: Top choice: Integration. Runner-up: Unit. Rejected Unit because behavior explicitly requires real data access: "stored in a local SQLite database" and "returns the correct pre-stored translation". Query/schema/encoding mismatches are DB-boundary failures that mocks can miss. Rejected System because local DB wiring is covered by Integration per level definition.
behaviors.test_description: Run fallback translator against a test SQLite DB seeded with known phrase pairs; assert exact phrase lookup results for present keys.

behaviors.description: The quality tracking comparison correctly computes edit distance between primary and fallback outputs and stores the metric.
behaviors.justification: Top choice: Integration. Runner-up: Unit. Rejected Unit because behavior includes persistence boundary: "computes edit distance ... and stores the metric". Even if distance math is unit-testable, this behavior’s real failure mode includes handoff to storage. Rejected Agentic because metric correctness is deterministic for given strings and does not require real model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Provide deterministic primary/fallback strings through the service path, verify computed character-level edit distance, and assert the metric is persisted in the configured store.

behaviors.description: When both translators are available, the service returns the primary translation and logs the quality comparison in the background.
behaviors.justification: Top choice: Integration. Runner-up: System. Rejected System because the behavior is precedence + async orchestration: "returns the primary translation" and "logs ... in the background"; this can be caught with in-process modules, controlled async executor, and local persistence. Rejected Agentic because assertions are about routing/order and side effects, not model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run service with both translator modules enabled (primary stub succeeds, fallback available), assert response uses primary output immediately, then assert background quality-log record is eventually written.
